explanation: This command modifies the registry by adding a value to the specified key. It uses insertion of quotes and short options to bypass the Sigma rule while maintaining the same behavior for testing purposes.
This command adds a value to the registry key without unnecessary parameters. It uses omission and reordering to simplify the command while still triggering the same behavior.
This command uses substitution of long options with short options (-d instead of /d, -f instead of /f) to alter the command structure while preserving its functionality.
